title: Streaming Analysis, Chunk Boundaries, and Resource Budgets · Language Clinic: Incremental Decoding
summary: A large-file mode still calls read_text, splits tokens at chunk edges, decodes partial UTF-8 incorrectly, retains every token, and claims bounded memory without measurement. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the incremental decoding increment in bounded streaming analyzer. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.
Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-streaming-bounded-memory-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-streaming-bounded-memory-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_streaming_bounded_memory_3(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-incremental-decoding-1h2fnt
# Competency: bookbot-incremental-decoding.
# Case 5974: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_incremental_decoding_1h2fnt(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1h2fnt = "1h2fnt-tnf2h1-5974"
    assert evidence_variant_1h2fnt
    evidence_axes_1h2fnt = (
        "corpus1h2fnt",
        "policytnf2h1",
        "revision5974",
        "command1h2fnt5974",
        "reporttnf2h15974",
        "test59741h2fnt",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1h2fnt)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Incremental Decoding model · Theory Incremental Decoding, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Incremental Decoding precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Incremental Decoding model · Theory Incremental Decoding, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 2637 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Incremental Decoding model · Theory Incremental Decoding, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: UTF-8 bytes may be split at any position and decoded separately.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-incremental-decoding-1h2fnt.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-incremental-decoding-1h2fnt(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_incremental_decoding_1h2fnt\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?chunks < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stream_tokens != batch_tokens)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?peak_bytes > memory_budget)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not fragments_closed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using incremental and decoder plus specific case evidence.
